kits.name: Gaming Room Build Kit
kits.description: UK-verified gaming room shopping list: three budget tiers, layout notes, cable plan, and decided Amazon carts — not another essay.
kits.honest_take: Pay for this when a compare already answered “which keyboard/mouse” and you want the full desk stack decided for a UK room (depth, ISO, neighbours). Skip if you only need one product — use the free compare pages.
kits.review_summary: Digital kit buyers (pattern from similar Garage Gym $29 kits) praise decided carts and tier clarity; complaints cluster on wanting live price sync and more ISO-UK SKUs. Our stack reviews: Wooting/Keychron HE boards win FPS praise but punish Synapse-haters less than Razer; Superlight 2 stays the boring reliable mouse; 27" QD-OLED (Alienware) is loved for motion and feared for burn-in on static Discord; Cloud III Wireless is the flat-noise default; Tapo plugs are the renter power kill-switch. Across the stack, owners praise ‘it just works on a 120cm desk’ and complain about RGB software bloat and OLED care anxiety.
who_for: UK gamers building a first proper desk in a bedroom or box room
People who finished 1–2 compares and want the rest of the cart decided
Flat dwellers who need closed-back audio and cable discipline
who_not_for: Anyone who only needs a single product recommendation (use free compares)
Collectors who already own HE boards + OLED and just want novelty
Buyers who refuse any affiliate links or Amazon UK stock
kits.setup_notes: Measure desk depth before the monitor; prefer a VESA arm on shallow UK desks. Run one power strip + Tapo on the PC/OLED for standby kill. Keep a quiet daytime typing profile if you share walls. Cable spine along the rear edge — not across the mousepad.

tier_labels.label: Starter
tier_labels.blurb: Playable desk: solid wireless mouse, closed headset, bias light, smart plug. Board can wait if budget is brutal.

tier_labels.label: Solid
tier_labels.blurb: HE/TKL board + light FPS mouse + 27" high-refresh panel + chair that lasts WFH days.

tier_labels.label: No compromise
tier_labels.blurb: Wooting-class HE, QD-OLED 27", planar or premium wireless audio, furniture-grade chair, full cable/power plan.

items.why_in_kit: Light, reliable FPS shape that does not demand a £200 board first — biggest aim upgrade per pound in the starter tier.
items.notes: Primary mouse across all tiers if shape fits.

items.why_in_kit: Closed cups for UK flats; mic good enough for Discord without Audeze money.
items.notes: Prefer over open-backs in terraces.

items.why_in_kit: Honest starter mechanical when HE budget is not there yet — graded 7.4 on purpose.
items.notes: Upgrade path → Keychron HE or Wooting.

items.why_in_kit: Bias/RGB vibe without Hue Sync Box tax; removable mounting for renters.
items.notes: Mind adhesive vs deposit.

items.why_in_kit: Renter-safe power kill + kWh monitoring for PC/OLED standby.
items.notes: Pack of 2–3 recommended.

items.why_in_kit: HE feel + wireless + work/game dual use — the UK ISO-friendly step before Wooting money.
items.notes: Solid-tier default board.

items.why_in_kit: Same mouse as starter — do not churn peripherals when upgrading the board/monitor.
items.notes: Keep if shape fits; else Viper V3 Pro.

items.why_in_kit: 27" high-refresh gaming panel that fits UK desk depth better than ultrawide.
items.notes: Enable pixel care; hide static overlays.

items.why_in_kit: Still the flat-default headset at this tier unless you upgrade to Maxwell.
Sub-£300 street chair that survives WFH + ranked without Secretlab tax.
items.notes: Measure stairs — it is heavy.

items.why_in_kit: Keep the kill-switch when the OLED arrives — standby adds up.
Rapid Trigger + F-row/arrows for work+game — practical peak HE without 60% purgatory.
items.notes: 60HE if you truly want max mouse space.

items.why_in_kit: Optional 60% path for competitive FPS on shallow desks — ANSI caveat for UK typists.
items.notes: Alt to 80HE, not both.

items.why_in_kit: Flatter/lighter competitive alternative when Superlight shape is wrong.
items.notes: Pick one mouse — Superlight or Viper.

items.why_in_kit: Keep 27" QD-OLED class panel; add arm + pixel hygiene.
items.notes: LG 27GR95QE if Alienware street price spikes.

items.why_in_kit: OLED alt when Alienware is silly money — budget a VESA arm.
items.notes: Alt monitor, not dual buy.

items.why_in_kit: Planar detail upgrade when sound is the bottleneck — heavy; not for every neck.
items.notes: Keep Cloud III if weight is a dealbreaker.

items.why_in_kit: Still the value furniture pick; spend the rest on panel/board not logo chairs.
Only if you already run Hue — graded honestly; Govee covers vibe cheaper.
items.notes: Optional cinema flex.

items.why_in_kit: Non-negotiable power hygiene when OLED + mini-PC/PS sit on one strip.

kits.name: UK Garage Gym Build Kit
kits.description: UK garage gym under 2.4m ceilings: rack/flooring/noise decisions, three budgets, and a decided cart list for single garages and terraces.
kits.honest_take: Buy when ceiling height and neighbour noise are the real constraints — not when you only need a dumbbell compare. Physical gear ASINs are thinner in our DB until Amazon sync returns; the kit still ships the decision logic + spoke links.
kits.review_summary: Garage-gym owners (UK forums + our spoke research) praise half-racks that fit single bays and hate optimistic ceiling claims. Recurring themes: rubber thickness for terraces, folding wall racks when the car still lives there, Mirafit value vs Rogue shipping pain, and adjustable dumbbells as the ≤£500 hero. Complaints: delivery damage on plates, rust in damp garages without heaters, and neighbours hearing drop noise through party walls.
who_for: UK single-garage or garden-room lifters under ~2.4m
Terrace/semi owners who must manage floor noise
Budget builders who want a cart, not a forum argument
who_not_for: People with a full double bay and unlimited Rogue budget who already know their rack
Apartment dwellers with no vibration plan (start with dumbbells only)
kits.setup_notes: Measure to the joists, not the brochure. Leave whip room for Olympic bars. Put 20mm+ rubber + underlay on party-wall floors before the rack. Bolt freestanding racks per manufacturer — UK concrete varies. Dehumidifier if tools rust in winter.

tier_labels.label: Bare bones
tier_labels.blurb: Adjustables + flooring start + pull-up alternative. No rack yet.

tier_labels.label: Working gym
tier_labels.blurb: Half rack or fold-away + rubber tiles + bench that fits width.

tier_labels.label: Full bay
tier_labels.blurb: Rack + plates + flooring stack + power/fan plan.

items.why_in_kit: Highest training ROI under £500 when a rack will not fit yet.
items.notes: ASIN pending Amazon sync — use compare for current UK picks.

items.why_in_kit: Terrace noise insurance — buy flooring before iron.
Cardio that will not get you a letter from next door.
UK-stock half rack that fits many single garages without Rogue freight drama.
items.notes: Confirm height vs 2.4m joists.

items.why_in_kit: When the car still wins weeknights — fold it, park it.
items.notes: Needs solid masonry/joist plan.

items.why_in_kit: Same noise stack — non-negotiable under a working rack.
Accessory volume without buying every dumbbell pair.
Anchor the bay; add plates/bench around it.
Know when Rogue freight is worth it vs Mirafit UK stock.
items.notes: Decision spoke, not a double rack buy.

items.why_in_kit: Ceiling/whip checklist before plates land.
Full-bay drops need the thick stack.
